evals.question_short: Row-level TTL — could PG support per-row auto-expire?

assertions.text: Output follows the 8-section structure (Problem / Why / Subsystems / Has-this-been-tried / Approaches / Recommend / DECISIONs / NOT-figured-out)
Names ≥2 relevant subsystem docs from knowledge/subsystems/* (e.g. access-heap, access-transam, parser-and-rewrite)
Sketches at least 2 genuinely distinct approaches (not 3 flavors of the same)
Each candidate approach has explicit Pros + Cons + Scope (small/medium/large)
Names existing prior art (pg_partman / pg_cron / Timescale / Citus, or CommitFest search result)
Consults the scenarios layer (knowledge/scenarios/_index.md) explicitly and names ≥1 scenario it would compose with
Picks ONE recommended approach with a reason (not 'all three are interesting')
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions only the user can answer (scope, semantics, core-vs-contrib, etc.)
Includes a 'What this brainstorm did NOT figure out' section drawing the boundary with Phase 2
Stays under ~300 lines AND does NOT drop file:line citations into source/ (Phase-2 work)
Tags uncertain claims as [unverified] (or equivalent honesty marker) rather than making them sound confident

evals.question_short: Online rewind to LSN X without restart

assertions.text: Output follows the 8-section structure
Names ≥2 relevant subsystems including access-transam (WAL/xlog/recovery) and either replication or storage-buffer
Identifies the existing PITR / recovery_target_lsn machinery as prior art
Identifies pg_rewind as an existing tool (and notes it's offline)
Sketches at least 2 genuinely distinct approaches (e.g. scripted restart vs in-place vs branchable snapshot)
Each candidate has Pros + Cons + Scope
Notes replication-slot semantics as a hard problem under rewind
Picks ONE recommended approach with reasoning + names what would have to be true for the others to win
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions (downtime tolerance, slot semantics, MVP scope, branchable-vs-rewind)
Flags scenarios-layer gap ('no scenario covers modifying the live recovery state machine')
Stays under ~300 lines AND does not drop file:line cites

evals.question_short: plpgsql_check as core — dead-code analysis at CREATE FUNCTION time

assertions.text: IDENTIFIES that the `plpgsql_check` extension by Pavel Stěhule already exists and provides this functionality (the key reframe)
Reframes the brainstorm as 'upstream vs make extension easier vs move to contrib' rather than designing from scratch
Names the existing `plpgsql_validator` hook in `pl_handler.c` and the `check_function_bodies` GUC as the existing surface
Sketches at least 2 distinct approaches that respect the prior-art reframe (not 3 ways to reinvent the extension)
Each candidate has Pros + Cons + Scope
Names ≥1 relevant scenario from knowledge/scenarios/_index.md (e.g. add-new-extension, add-new-guc) OR flags a scenarios gap
Picks ONE recommendation with reasoning
Surfaces a DECISION: that asks the user 'are you aware the extension exists' or equivalent — i.e. surfaces that the brainstorm itself may be moot
Surfaces ≥3 DECISION: questions total
Stays under ~300 lines AND does not drop file:line cites
Output follows the 8-section structure
